quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: A February 2024 Gazette recruitment rule names Administrative Assistant ‘B’ as a Level 4 post in the Central Pay Matrix (₹25,500–81,100 basic-pay range). This is pay-matrix context, not a guaranteed take-home salary: allowances, location, deductions, and future government revisions can change actual pay.

sections.heading: How the post is reached
paragraphs: Administrative Assistant ‘B’ is best understood as an internal DRDO administrative-cadre progression point, not the usual public entry post. In the 2011 statutory schedule, 75% of vacancies were to be filled by promotion from Administrative Assistant ‘A’ with eight years’ regular service in the relevant DPC-II group; the remaining 25% were through a Limited Departmental Competitive Examination (LDCE) route for eligible Group ‘C’ personnel with the stated educational and service conditions.

The route has been amended over time. In September 2013, DRDO’s amendment changed the designation of the post from a selection to a non-selection post and replaced “selection” with “seniority” in the recruitment method; it also retained a five-year eligibility concession for certain people already holding Administrative Assistant ‘A’ on the notification date. Accordingly, an employee should rely on the recruitment rules, departmental orders, seniority position, vacancy year, and DPC/LDCE notice actually applicable when promotion is considered rather than assuming that the original eight-year condition applies unchanged.

For an external candidate, the practical starting point has historically been Administrative Assistant ‘A’. For example, DRDO’s CEPTAM-09 Admin & Allied notice required 12th-class completion plus a computer typing test—35 words per minute in English or 30 words per minute in Hindi—for Administrative Assistant ‘A’. That notice is dated 2019 and is evidence of a prior entry route, not a current recruitment notification.
bullets: Join DRDO through an officially advertised eligible feeder or Admin & Allied role.
Build regular service, satisfactory service record, and eligibility under the then-applicable departmental rules.
Monitor DRDO/CEPTAM notices and internal establishment communications for promotion, DPC, or LDCE instructions.

sections.heading: Skills that support progression
paragraphs: The statutory rules do not publish a detailed duty statement for Administrative Assistant ‘B’. However, the documented feeder and entry pathway makes strong written communication, accurate computer-based typing, records discipline, familiarity with government-office procedures, and careful handling of service and establishment documentation relevant foundations. The historical Administrative Assistant ‘A’ notice specifically assessed computer typing in English or Hindi, while DRDO’s HR function identifies establishment-policy compliance, recruitment, compensation, performance assessment, and training among its responsibilities.

For promotion readiness, employees should develop capability in interpreting applicable orders, maintaining accurate files and registers, drafting clear official communications, protecting sensitive official information, and working reliably through prescribed administrative processes. These are career-development priorities inferred from DRDO’s administrative and HR operating context; precise duties can vary by laboratory, establishment, and assignment.
bullets: Computer typing and document preparation in the applicable language stream.
Accuracy in records, correspondence, and establishment documentation.
Working knowledge of applicable government and DRDO administrative procedures.
Professional communication, confidentiality, and process compliance.

sections.heading: Work setting and posting
paragraphs: Administrative Assistant ‘B’ belongs to DRDO’s administrative structure, which supports a defence research organisation operating laboratories, establishments, and units across India. DRDO states that its HR cluster handles such areas as recruitment, compensation, training, performance assessment, establishment-policy formulation, and compliance with Government of India guidelines.

A historical CEPTAM Admin & Allied recruitment notice stated that DRDO had more than 60 laboratories, establishments, and units across the country; it also stated that selected personnel could be posted anywhere in India, including field or remote locations, according to organisational requirements. Because that notice was issued in 2019, it should be read as service-condition context rather than a current posting guarantee.
bullets: Work may be in a DRDO laboratory, establishment, unit, or headquarters-linked administrative environment.
Posting and transfer are organisational decisions and may involve locations across India.
The work is governed by central-government service rules and DRDO establishment processes.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: For current pay-scale context, a February 2024 official Gazette rule lists Administrative Assistant ‘B’ at Pay Matrix Level 4, ₹25,500–81,100. This is the basic-pay matrix range, not an all-inclusive monthly salary or an assurance of a particular salary on promotion.

A DRDO Admin & Allied notice explains that central-government compensation may also include applicable dearness allowance, house-rent allowance, transport allowance, medical facilities, leave travel concession, and other benefits under Government of India rules; it also stated that recruits were covered by the National Pension System unless otherwise provided. That notice concerns 2019 recruitment and its benefits language is therefore contextual and subject to subsequent rules, posting location, and individual eligibility.
bullets: Reference basic-pay range: Level 4, ₹25,500–81,100, as identified in a February 2024 Gazette rule.
Take-home pay varies with allowances, city/posting category, pension contributions, tax, and government revisions.
Confirm current pay fixation and allowances with the relevant DRDO establishment office when promoted.
